zotero.abstractNote: Continual learning (CL) aims to enable neural networks to learn from a stream of data without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. However, evaluating CL methods is often hindered by the lack of suitable benchmarks that allow for a clear distinction between memorization and generalization. In this work, we introduce Infinite dSprites (IdSprites), a novel, procedurally generated dataset designed to facilitate the study of disentangled representations in CL. IdSprites provides a controlled environment where the underlying factors of variation are known, allowing us to precisely measure how well a model generalizes to unseen combinations of factors versus how much it relies on memorizing specific samples. We use IdSprites to evaluate several state-of-the-art CL methods and show that they often struggle to disentangle the factors of variation, leading to poor generalization and a reliance on memorization. Our findings highlight the importance of disentanglement in CL and provide a new benchmark for future research in this direction.
